texts.label: text
texts.orig: Hereon flat sheet membrane
texts.text: Hereon flat sheet membrane

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: “PolyActive” used as a separation layer of the CC membrane of Hereon is developed and produced by PolyVation (a Dutch company specialized in polymer material). Only a small quantity is necessary for the membrane production (80 kg to produce 400 000 m2 of membrane). If there is a threat in the sourcing of PolyActive material, it can be replaced by an alternative similar material.
texts.text: “PolyActive” used as a separation layer of the CC membrane of Hereon is developed and produced by PolyVation (a Dutch company specialized in polymer material). Only a small quantity is necessary for the membrane production (80 kg to produce 400 000 m2 of membrane). If there is a threat in the sourcing of PolyActive material, it can be replaced by an alternative similar material.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: PolyActive is composed of 77 wt % of poly(ethylene glycol terephthalate) and 23 wt % of poly(butylene terephthalate).
texts.text: PolyActive is composed of 77 wt % of poly(ethylene glycol terephthalate) and 23 wt % of poly(butylene terephthalate).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Hereon has decided not to file any patent related to PolyActive gas membrane, because it is not possible to protect it fully with a patent and all the secrets rely on the manufacturing know-how which Hereon wishes not to disclose in a patent.
texts.text: Hereon has decided not to file any patent related to PolyActive gas membrane, because it is not possible to protect it fully with a patent and all the secrets rely on the manufacturing know-how which Hereon wishes not to disclose in a patent.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Module (= housing with membrane cartridges)
texts.text: Module (= housing with membrane cartridges)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The housing is designed to resist up to 10 bara although the usual operation pressure is less than 5 bara. It is built in SS 316 and needs to carry the weight of 2-8 membrane cartridges. The membrane cartridges are not pressure containing.
texts.text: The housing is designed to resist up to 10 bara although the usual operation pressure is less than 5 bara. It is built in SS 316 and needs to carry the weight of 2-8 membrane cartridges. The membrane cartridges are not pressure containing.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Each membrane cartridge will have 200 membranes for Phase 2a, then 266 membranes from Phase 2b.
texts.text: Each membrane cartridge will have 200 membranes for Phase 2a, then 266 membranes from Phase 2b.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Each
texts.text: Each

texts.label: text
texts.orig: membrane cartridge has a total membrane surface of 256 m2 (from Phase 2b)
texts.text: membrane cartridge has a total membrane surface of 256 m2 (from Phase 2b)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: . One module with 2 membrane cartridges weights 2,5 tons.
texts.text: . One module with 2 membrane cartridges weights 2,5 tons.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Maintenance operation (mostly the replacement of membrane cartridges) will be carried out every 5 years by lifting and transporting the whole module with cartridges in a dedicated external facility (to be built).
texts.text: Maintenance operation (mostly the replacement of membrane cartridges) will be carried out every 5 years by lifting and transporting the whole module with cartridges in a dedicated external facility (to be built).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For larger installations (Holcim Phase 2a or 3), up to 4 modules could be installed in a 40 feet ISO container as a standard unit.
texts.text: For larger installations (Holcim Phase 2a or 3), up to 4 modules could be installed in a 40 feet ISO container as a standard unit.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Flat sheet membrane cartridge vs spiral type membranes
texts.text: Flat sheet membrane cartridge vs spiral type membranes

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Considering that CPT has spent 3 years (since its establishment in 2019) to develop the design concept based on modules of flat sheet membrane cartridge, they strongly believe that their design is superior in terms of cost, foot print, complexity, … to spiral type membrane architecture for large CC facilities. In addition, spiral type membranes would be difficult to suit with the CPT vacuum assisted process. Hereon has built a prototype spiral type membrane, so we could witness its manufacturing feasibility.
texts.text: Considering that CPT has spent 3 years (since its establishment in 2019) to develop the design concept based on modules of flat sheet membrane cartridge, they strongly believe that their design is superior in terms of cost, foot print, complexity, … to spiral type membrane architecture for large CC facilities. In addition, spiral type membranes would be difficult to suit with the CPT vacuum assisted process. Hereon has built a prototype spiral type membrane, so we could witness its manufacturing feasibility.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Operation of Spiral wound membranes under vacuum to be validated.
texts.text: Operation of Spiral wound membranes under vacuum to be validated.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: However, membrane envelope fabrication process (fine piling of membranes with spacers, then electric welding), then piling of the membrane envelopes on CO2 collector pipes with placement of numerous joints will make these process extremely difficult or costly to automatize with perspective of high labor cost in Germany.
texts.text: However, membrane envelope fabrication process (fine piling of membranes with spacers, then electric welding), then piling of the membrane envelopes on CO2 collector pipes with placement of numerous joints will make these process extremely difficult or costly to automatize with perspective of high labor cost in Germany.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: According to CPT, typical spiral tubular type housing will have 40-80 m2 of membrane surface against 256 m2 for flat sheet membrane cartridge.
texts.text: According to CPT, typical spiral tubular type housing will have 40-80 m2 of membrane surface against 256 m2 for flat sheet membrane cartridge.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: WTS has one RO membrane production facility (ex Lanxess) in Germany (near Bitterfeld/Leipzig) and a part of RO manufacturing machine tools could be reused for the manufacturing of CC gas membranes (this is under investigation. WTS is now checking what kind of additional machine tools will be necessary to the existing equipment). However, we should seriously reconsider the validity of spiral type CC gas membrane WTS since we should be able to reuse the existing fully automated and cost efficient process for spiral type membrane manufacturing with an established long learning curve. If this is the direction WTS takes to minimize Capex for a manufacturing facility taking advantage of its proven track record, it might conflict with the development of CPT on flat sheet membrane cartridge type configuration.
texts.text: WTS has one RO membrane production facility (ex Lanxess) in Germany (near Bitterfeld/Leipzig) and a part of RO manufacturing machine tools could be reused for the manufacturing of CC gas membranes (this is under investigation. WTS is now checking what kind of additional machine tools will be necessary to the existing equipment). However, we should seriously reconsider the validity of spiral type CC gas membrane WTS since we should be able to reuse the existing fully automated and cost efficient process for spiral type membrane manufacturing with an established long learning curve. If this is the direction WTS takes to minimize Capex for a manufacturing facility taking advantage of its proven track record, it might conflict with the development of CPT on flat sheet membrane cartridge type configuration.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CPT Herstellung GmbH (future membrane production plant)
texts.text: CPT Herstellung GmbH (future membrane production plant)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPT Herstellung is now 100% CPT with an option for Hereon to acquire 10% of the shares.
texts.text: CPT Herstellung is now 100% CPT with an option for Hereon to acquire 10% of the shares.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The future membrane production facility will be designed
texts.text: The future membrane production facility will be designed

texts.label: text
texts.orig: to produce around 380 000 m2 of membrane in 3 shifts
texts.text: to produce around 380 000 m2 of membrane in 3 shifts

texts.label: text
texts.orig: with a total of 48 operators.
texts.text: with a total of 48 operators.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The target production cost will be between 40-50 €/m2 without indirect costs (management and administration costs,..). The breakdown provided in the data room shows 27€/m2 for material, 15€ for labor, 1,4€ for energy, but more detailed cost breakdown will be provided soon by CPT.
texts.text: The target production cost will be between 40-50 €/m2 without indirect costs (management and administration costs,..). The breakdown provided in the data room shows 27€/m2 for material, 15€ for labor, 1,4€ for energy, but more detailed cost breakdown will be provided soon by CPT.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPT confirmed the future location of CPT Herstellung is not decided yet. The initial idea was to set the plant in Rostock (about 180 km nord east of Hamburg where Hereon is located) aiming 20-30% of Capex subsidies from local government. However, CPT is now considering seriously a location in the south of Austria where subsidies would be much more significant although more complex in term of logistic with Hereon.
texts.text: CPT confirmed the future location of CPT Herstellung is not decided yet. The initial idea was to set the plant in Rostock (about 180 km nord east of Hamburg where Hereon is located) aiming 20-30% of Capex subsidies from local government. However, CPT is now considering seriously a location in the south of Austria where subsidies would be much more significant although more complex in term of logistic with Hereon.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: The size of the plant would be 50 m x 50 m. Layout, machine tool list, automatization level, phase ramp up schedule, detailed Capex, etc.. are requested.
texts.text: The size of the plant would be 50 m x 50 m. Layout, machine tool list, automatization level, phase ramp up schedule, detailed Capex, etc.. are requested.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: There is no written obligation, but if Veolia is considering the manufacturing of the membranes, it is highly recommended by CPT to do it in Germany. Indeed, Hereon has used German public fund to develop CC gas membrane and is encouraged to benefit German industry in addition to the collection of license fees.
texts.text: There is no written obligation, but if Veolia is considering the manufacturing of the membranes, it is highly recommended by CPT to do it in Germany. Indeed, Hereon has used German public fund to develop CC gas membrane and is encouraged to benefit German industry in addition to the collection of license fees.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Considering the long delivery time of customized machine tools in Germany, it is expected that the delivery time of the machines tools would take 18 months. If we had the time necessary to plan and design the plant, order the machines, then install and commission, it would be reasonable to estimate the delivery time of a minimum 3 years. This means that even if CPT has the funding (30 M€ ? depending on the level of automation. to be checked) to start the plant today, the first batch of membrane produced by CPT Herstellung won’t be available before the end of 2026 (but most probably by end 2027 at the earliest, because CPT is still far from securing the Capex of membrane facility).
texts.text: Considering the long delivery time of customized machine tools in Germany, it is expected that the delivery time of the machines tools would take 18 months. If we had the time necessary to plan and design the plant, order the machines, then install and commission, it would be reasonable to estimate the delivery time of a minimum 3 years. This means that even if CPT has the funding (30 M€ ? depending on the level of automation. to be checked) to start the plant today, the first batch of membrane produced by CPT Herstellung won’t be available before the end of 2026 (but most probably by end 2027 at the earliest, because CPT is still far from securing the Capex of membrane facility).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPT is planning to use the membranes produced in CPT Herstellung from Holcim project Phase 2b (supposed to start late 2024), but no membrane will be available before the end of 2027, therefore delaying the whole project to the end of 2028.
texts.text: CPT is planning to use the membranes produced in CPT Herstellung from Holcim project Phase 2b (supposed to start late 2024), but no membrane will be available before the end of 2027, therefore delaying the whole project to the end of 2028.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: IP & License contract between CPT and Hereon
texts.text: IP & License contract between CPT and Hereon

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: A Spin-off company (75% CPT, 25% Hereon) will hold IP related to PolyActive membrane ?? (to be checked)
texts.text: A Spin-off company (75% CPT, 25% Hereon) will hold IP related to PolyActive membrane ?? (to be checked)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: For each m2 of membrane sold, CPT has to pay 1,5€ of royalty to Hereon according to the license contract signed in April 2023.
texts.text: For each m2 of membrane sold, CPT has to pay 1,5€ of royalty to Hereon according to the license contract signed in April 2023.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: IP of housing is owned 30% by CPT and 70% by Hereon according to the Patent+License Agreement signed in June 2023. For each housing (whatever the size: 2 or 8 cartridges), CPT has to pay 5 000-7 500 € (depending on the type of housing) of royalty to Hereon. A minimum license fee of 50 000 €/y will be applied from January 2026, then increased progressively to 200 000€/y. (to be checked by Naomi)
texts.text: IP of housing is owned 30% by CPT and 70% by Hereon according to the Patent+License Agreement signed in June 2023. For each housing (whatever the size: 2 or 8 cartridges), CPT has to pay 5 000-7 500 € (depending on the type of housing) of royalty to Hereon. A minimum license fee of 50 000 €/y will be applied from January 2026, then increased progressively to 200 000€/y. (to be checked by Naomi)

texts.text: Other licensees of Hereon

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: GMT (Borsig Membrane has acquired 51% of GMT) does not use PolyActive type membrane but has the right to manufacture it.
texts.text: GMT (Borsig Membrane has acquired 51% of GMT) does not use PolyActive type membrane but has the right to manufacture it.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Jütlich (part of Helmholtz Zentrum, but a separate level entity from membrane Dept) is also an internal licensee, but their role in CC seems minor.
texts.text: Jütlich (part of Helmholtz Zentrum, but a separate level entity from membrane Dept) is also an internal licensee, but their role in CC seems minor.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2 other licensees are Flowserve and Avid Technologies (for US market) are mainly working since more than 20 years on car/truck gasoline vapor recovery equipment of relatively small size. They are not involved nor interested in the CC market.
texts.text: 2 other licensees are Flowserve and Avid Technologies (for US market) are mainly working since more than 20 years on car/truck gasoline vapor recovery equipment of relatively small size. They are not involved nor interested in the CC market.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Other than GMT and Borsig Membrane, CPT claims that they are the exclusive licensee of Hereon on the CC market.
texts.text: Other than GMT and Borsig Membrane, CPT claims that they are the exclusive licensee of Hereon on the CC market.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Holcim projects
texts.text: Holcim projects

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: They are 4 planned pilot+industrial phases with Holcim:
texts.text: They are 4 planned pilot+industrial phases with Holcim:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Phase 1 (45 t of CO2/y requiring 12 m2 of membrane surface):
texts.text: Phase 1 (45 t of CO2/y requiring 12 m2 of membrane surface):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This demonstration phase with pilot test is successfully completed during 2023. This is a 10 year old Hereon pilot unit revamped for the purpose of Holcim test. The unit returned to Hereon site.
texts.text: This demonstration phase with pilot test is successfully completed during 2023. This is a 10 year old Hereon pilot unit revamped for the purpose of Holcim test. The unit returned to Hereon site.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Phase 2a (6 000-10 000 t of CO2/y requiring 540 m2 of membrane surface):
texts.text: Phase 2a (6 000-10 000 t of CO2/y requiring 540 m2 of membrane surface):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Design is over and purchasing orders of key equipment are done. The assembly and delivery of the whole skids will be performed in the first half of 2024. The commissioning is expected by mid 2024 with the end of the test campaign by the end of 2024. Today, due to the lack of CPT funding, the whole Phase 2a seems in halt until the beginning of next year corresponding to the timing of capital increase with arrival of Technip and Veolia. CPT has explained that Technip has invested recently in another membrane company and they would have compliance issues investing in CPT. Therefore, Technip who was supposed to invest during the first half of 2023 has delayed the decision to 2024 until compliance issues are clarified (Pablo will check this topic directly with his contact in Technip. M. Gaultier?). There is a high probability that the phase 2a is further delayed if CPT is facing harder cash flow issues. The budget for Phase 2a would be around 4,6 M€. Phase 2a will use membranes manufactured by Hereon (0,6 m x 1,3 m, 0,72 m2 of surface, 200 per membrane cartridge -> 144 m2 of membrane surface per cartridge)
texts.text: The Design is over and purchasing orders of key equipment are done. The assembly and delivery of the whole skids will be performed in the first half of 2024. The commissioning is expected by mid 2024 with the end of the test campaign by the end of 2024. Today, due to the lack of CPT funding, the whole Phase 2a seems in halt until the beginning of next year corresponding to the timing of capital increase with arrival of Technip and Veolia. CPT has explained that Technip has invested recently in another membrane company and they would have compliance issues investing in CPT. Therefore, Technip who was supposed to invest during the first half of 2023 has delayed the decision to 2024 until compliance issues are clarified (Pablo will check this topic directly with his contact in Technip. M. Gaultier?). There is a high probability that the phase 2a is further delayed if CPT is facing harder cash flow issues. The budget for Phase 2a would be around 4,6 M€. Phase 2a will use membranes manufactured by Hereon (0,6 m x 1,3 m, 0,72 m2 of surface, 200 per membrane cartridge -> 144 m2 of membrane surface per cartridge)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Phase 2b (200 000 t of CO2/y requiring 16 000 m2 of membrane surface):
texts.text: Phase 2b (200 000 t of CO2/y requiring 16 000 m2 of membrane surface):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This phase is supposed to start upon successful conclusion of Phase 2a, but CPT claims that they are confident that the design works will start much earlier (probably by mid 2024). In this Phase, a module of 8 membrane cartridges with a total membrane surface of 2 000 m2 will be used to capture 25 000 t of CO2/y. 2 ISO containers with 4 modules in each container will bring the capacity to 200 000 t/y. Phase 2b and 3 will use membranes manufactured by CPT Herstellung (0,8 m x 1,2 m, 0,96 m2, 266 membranes per cartridge -> 256 m2 of membrane per cartridge). MTR is presently building a plant of 55 000 t of CO2 capture for a Capex of 85 M$, so we can estimate that Phase 2b could have a budget of at least 50-60 M€ even if CPT design is more compact and less expensive as claimed by them.
texts.text: This phase is supposed to start upon successful conclusion of Phase 2a, but CPT claims that they are confident that the design works will start much earlier (probably by mid 2024). In this Phase, a module of 8 membrane cartridges with a total membrane surface of 2 000 m2 will be used to capture 25 000 t of CO2/y. 2 ISO containers with 4 modules in each container will bring the capacity to 200 000 t/y. Phase 2b and 3 will use membranes manufactured by CPT Herstellung (0,8 m x 1,2 m, 0,96 m2, 266 membranes per cartridge -> 256 m2 of membrane per cartridge). MTR is presently building a plant of 55 000 t of CO2 capture for a Capex of 85 M$, so we can estimate that Phase 2b could have a budget of at least 50-60 M€ even if CPT design is more compact and less expensive as claimed by them.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Phase 3 (1 Mt of CO2/y requiring 80 000 m2 of membrane surface):
texts.text: Phase 3 (1 Mt of CO2/y requiring 80 000 m2 of membrane surface):

texts.label: text
texts.orig: this is a real industrial step, but once Phase 2a is validated, CPT will reuse existing standard modules and containers to increase the plant capacity (10 containers filled with modules).
texts.text: this is a real industrial step, but once Phase 2a is validated, CPT will reuse existing standard modules and containers to increase the plant capacity (10 containers filled with modules).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Comparison with MTR
texts.text: Comparison with MTR

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Hereon considers that MTR membranes are very similar in terms of performance to Hereon membranes. However the operation pressure of MTR is around 1,4-1,5 bara which is much lower than CPT’s 4,5 bara. CPT claims that the high pressure operation allows them to reduce the footprint of the plant by a factor of 4, and reduce the Capex by 50%. In addition, 1 CPT designed housing will be equivalent to 30 MTR housing (to be checked).
texts.text: Hereon considers that MTR membranes are very similar in terms of performance to Hereon membranes. However the operation pressure of MTR is around 1,4-1,5 bara which is much lower than CPT’s 4,5 bara. CPT claims that the high pressure operation allows them to reduce the footprint of the plant by a factor of 4, and reduce the Capex by 50%. In addition, 1 CPT designed housing will be equivalent to 30 MTR housing (to be checked).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Manufacturing analysis
texts.text: Manufacturing analysis

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane manufacturing
texts.text: Membrane manufacturing

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Hereon’s membrane is a thin-film composite, consisting of a polyacrylonitrile (PAN) support membrane coated on a polyester non-woven. Both are known to WTS as PAN membrane used to be produced in MTKA plant in the past. PAN concentration is 20% using a known solvent (won’t be disclosed), air gap 120-150 um, coated using a comma coater at 4 m/min (current BTF UF support production is at 20 m/min) and <60% humidity. Support membrane is coated on a pilot coater with a max (coated) width and length of 60 cm x 250m.
texts.text: Hereon’s membrane is a thin-film composite, consisting of a polyacrylonitrile (PAN) support membrane coated on a polyester non-woven. Both are known to WTS as PAN membrane used to be produced in MTKA plant in the past. PAN concentration is 20% using a known solvent (won’t be disclosed), air gap 120-150 um, coated using a comma coater at 4 m/min (current BTF UF support production is at 20 m/min) and <60% humidity. Support membrane is coated on a pilot coater with a max (coated) width and length of 60 cm x 250m.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Next step is hot water bath washing at 70°C to wash out volatile solvent at 0.5 m/min using a separate machine. This is seen as the bottleneck step of membrane manufacturing due to the low speed.
texts.text: Next step is hot water bath washing at 70°C to wash out volatile solvent at 0.5 m/min using a separate machine. This is seen as the bottleneck step of membrane manufacturing due to the low speed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Third step is to coat PDMS (150 nm), PolyActive (85 nm) and another PDMS layer (150 nm), respectively, on a thin film coater at 0.8 m/min. Each coat has to be done separately due to limitation of the coater (three-pass). Roller coating using 2 rollers are used. Meniscus between the rollers determines the coating thickness (also viscosity of coating solution and coating speed). TFC coater includes roller coating, air knife, hot air to blow out solvent, followed by crosslinking in an oven.
texts.text: Third step is to coat PDMS (150 nm), PolyActive (85 nm) and another PDMS layer (150 nm), respectively, on a thin film coater at 0.8 m/min. Each coat has to be done separately due to limitation of the coater (three-pass). Roller coating using 2 rollers are used. Meniscus between the rollers determines the coating thickness (also viscosity of coating solution and coating speed). TFC coater includes roller coating, air knife, hot air to blow out solvent, followed by crosslinking in an oven.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2 FTEs at Hereon are producing the membrane (not full time). Currently 1 roll of PolyActive membrane (250 m) is produced at a rate of 60 manhour.
texts.text: 2 FTEs at Hereon are producing the membrane (not full time). Currently 1 roll of PolyActive membrane (250 m) is produced at a rate of 60 manhour.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Observation
texts.text: Observation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: : membrane manufacturing seems to be simple enough to scale up (RO membrane manufacturing has higher complexity) but need to check whether PolyActive coating is limiting the coating speed or the slow coating speed is due to limitations of the pilot coater or lack of optimization by Hereon. We have knowledge of producing PAN/UF support membranes at a higher speed. Coating step could be improved by having a series of coaters and ovens but will require a new CAPEX. Another option is to go for toll manufacturing.
texts.text: : membrane manufacturing seems to be simple enough to scale up (RO membrane manufacturing has higher complexity) but need to check whether PolyActive coating is limiting the coating speed or the slow coating speed is due to limitations of the pilot coater or lack of optimization by Hereon. We have knowledge of producing PAN/UF support membranes at a higher speed. Coating step could be improved by having a series of coaters and ovens but will require a new CAPEX. Another option is to go for toll manufacturing.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Raw materials
texts.text: Raw materials

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Due to the simplicity of their recipe (other than unknown solvents, which Hereon claimed to be common), it is relatively easy to source their raw materials. PAN, PDMS, PE non-woven are all common materials for flat sheet membrane production, at which WTS already has channels. PolyActive is purchased from PolyVation which is currently their single source. No other known supplier sells PolyActive and CPT does not have any exclusivity to purchase PolyActive (amount needed per year is too small – 80 kg). This might be problematic since PolyActive is the key ingredient of the membrane. CPT claimed that PolyVation does not own the IP of PolyActive (might already be expired) and the synthesis of the compound seems simple. CPT will have a discussion with PolyVation in 2 weeks regarding securing PolyActive supply (need to follow up).
texts.text: Due to the simplicity of their recipe (other than unknown solvents, which Hereon claimed to be common), it is relatively easy to source their raw materials. PAN, PDMS, PE non-woven are all common materials for flat sheet membrane production, at which WTS already has channels. PolyActive is purchased from PolyVation which is currently their single source. No other known supplier sells PolyActive and CPT does not have any exclusivity to purchase PolyActive (amount needed per year is too small – 80 kg). This might be problematic since PolyActive is the key ingredient of the membrane. CPT claimed that PolyVation does not own the IP of PolyActive (might already be expired) and the synthesis of the compound seems simple. CPT will have a discussion with PolyVation in 2 weeks regarding securing PolyActive supply (need to follow up).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Quality assurance
texts.text: Quality assurance

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Quality assurance is done on the membrane sheet (only PolyActive, no control on PAN), membrane envelope and membrane cartridge. Samples of membrane sheets are taken every few 10ms during coating and measured for permeate flux (bench equipment). Current scrap rate is 10%. Most common defect is due to PolyActive layer not coated evenly. Test time is 2-5 mins.
texts.text: Quality assurance is done on the membrane sheet (only PolyActive, no control on PAN), membrane envelope and membrane cartridge. Samples of membrane sheets are taken every few 10ms during coating and measured for permeate flux (bench equipment). Current scrap rate is 10%. Most common defect is due to PolyActive layer not coated evenly. Test time is 2-5 mins.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Every membrane envelope is tested for leak using pressurized air. Current scrap rate it 10%. Test time is 2-5 mins.
texts.text: Every membrane envelope is tested for leak using pressurized air. Current scrap rate it 10%. Test time is 2-5 mins.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Membrane cartridges will be tested for leakage using pressurized air and vacuum. Test time ca. 30 mins.
texts.text: Membrane cartridges will be tested for leakage using pressurized air and vacuum. Test time ca. 30 mins.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: No chemicals were involved in quality testing, only bench tests using pressurized air
texts.text: No chemicals were involved in quality testing, only bench tests using pressurized air

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane envelope and cartridge
texts.text: Membrane envelope and cartridge

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Membrane sheets are cut using a machine cutter (made specially for the size and shape of envelope). Envelope consists of 2 sheets of membrane, 2 permeate spacer (seems like a standard PET tricot) and a non-woven. Envelope is welded using heat in another machine and assembled/stacked similar to ED into the cartridge. All steps are done manually. 1 cartridge currently takes 2 weeks to assemble with 1 FTE not working full time.
texts.text: Membrane sheets are cut using a machine cutter (made specially for the size and shape of envelope). Envelope consists of 2 sheets of membrane, 2 permeate spacer (seems like a standard PET tricot) and a non-woven. Envelope is welded using heat in another machine and assembled/stacked similar to ED into the cartridge. All steps are done manually. 1 cartridge currently takes 2 weeks to assemble with 1 FTE not working full time.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: No glue was needed in assembly
texts.text: No glue was needed in assembly

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Module
texts.text: Module

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Module is cylindrical shape of stainless-steel tube. One module consists of 4-8 cartridges (4 preferred). One container consists of 32 cartridges. One cartridge is around 140 m2 of membrane. Module manufacturing is subtracted to a local builder (basically any builder that has license for welding and working with pressurized vessels can build the module). TüV certification is provided for every module. Delivery time is 8 weeks for 2 modules.
texts.text: Module is cylindrical shape of stainless-steel tube. One module consists of 4-8 cartridges (4 preferred). One container consists of 32 cartridges. One cartridge is around 140 m2 of membrane. Module manufacturing is subtracted to a local builder (basically any builder that has license for welding and working with pressurized vessels can build the module). TüV certification is provided for every module. Delivery time is 8 weeks for 2 modules.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Module development: (1) rectangular type (developed together with FH Jülich) single envelope, IP owned by Hereon, (2) envelope type module, which is used in the small pilot unit (Memkor), (3) currently filing a patent that is rectangular type but stacked and fitted in a pressure vessel (joint with CPT). Stainless steel designed for 10 bar but can still be optimized.
texts.text: Module development: (1) rectangular type (developed together with FH Jülich) single envelope, IP owned by Hereon, (2) envelope type module, which is used in the small pilot unit (Memkor), (3) currently filing a patent that is rectangular type but stacked and fitted in a pressure vessel (joint with CPT). Stainless steel designed for 10 bar but can still be optimized.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Cost
texts.text: Cost

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPT claims the manufacturing cost of membrane to be EUR 45/m2 at a selling price of EUR 200/m2 (need to check if too optimistic)
texts.text: CPT claims the manufacturing cost of membrane to be EUR 45/m2 at a selling price of EUR 200/m2 (need to check if too optimistic)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Production plant
texts.text: Production plant

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: CPT would like to copy Hereon’s lab coater/manufacturing facility 1:1 for their production plant, except for the width (up to 1m). This is not feasible for reducing the production cost, considering the current coating speed. They have not investigated other coating methods (jet, spray, etc.) which might be more suitable for higher speed coatings. This is the area where WTS can help considering our knowledge in membrane coating. It is very clear that CPT and Hereon do not have the manufacturing experience (and have not engaged contractors familiar with the design and fabrication of coating equipment) to successfully scale up membrane and cartridge manufacturing
texts.text: CPT would like to copy Hereon’s lab coater/manufacturing facility 1:1 for their production plant, except for the width (up to 1m). This is not feasible for reducing the production cost, considering the current coating speed. It is very clear that CPT and Hereon do not have the manufacturing experience (and have not engaged contractors familiar with the design and fabrication of coating equipment) to successfully scale up membrane and cartridge manufacturing

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane aging/degradation
texts.text: Membrane aging/degradation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Membrane aging has never been tested directly. It is understood that the PolyActive membrane can be degraded due to oxidation (chemical aging), which has never been observed and due to deposition of contamination (liquid, particles, etc.), which can be avoided with proper feed pre-treatment.
texts.text: Membrane aging has never been tested directly. It is understood that the PolyActive membrane can be degraded due to oxidation (chemical aging), which has never been observed and due to deposition of contamination (liquid, particles, etc.), which can be avoided with proper feed pre-treatment.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In a separate funded project (SSC), PolyActive membrane was tested for 1 year of operation for capturing CO2 during natural gas burning (very clean feed). This was simulated in a building in Hamburg to enrich flue gas for algae cultivation. Flue gas contained 9% of CO2 and had to be enriched to 45%. When contamination due to soot and dust accumulation occurred, membrane performance degraded (CO2 output down from 45% to 30% conc.). This was solved by adding a filter (activated carbon) as a pre-treatment. Membrane performance was claimed to be back to initial value. Important to note that the module used was the envelope type (2
texts.text: In a separate funded project (SSC), PolyActive membrane was tested for 1 year of operation for capturing CO2 during natural gas burning (very clean feed). This was simulated in a building in Hamburg to enrich flue gas for algae cultivation. Flue gas contained 9% of CO2 and had to be enriched to 45%. When contamination due to soot and dust accumulation occurred, membrane performance degraded (CO2 output down from 45% to 30% conc.). This was solved by adding a filter (activated carbon) as a pre-treatment. Membrane performance was claimed to be back to initial value. Important to note that the module used was the envelope type (2

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Future products
texts.text: Future products

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: When asked which direction Hereon should go with regard to product improvement, they pointed out that the short term goal would be to increase the permeability of the current PolyActive membrane. This can be achieved by changing the non-woven (presumably using a thinner one), changing the thickness and/or polymer of the support membrane (PAN only has 8-13% porosity), as well as faster manufacturing rate. Another possibility would be to replace the active material (PolyActive) with another polymer.
texts.text: When asked which direction Hereon should go with regard to product improvement, they pointed out that the short term goal would be to increase the permeability of the current PolyActive membrane. This can be achieved by changing the non-woven (presumably using a thinner one), changing the thickness and/or polymer of the support membrane (PAN only has 8-13% porosity), as well as faster manufacturing rate. Another possibility would be to replace the active material (PolyActive) with another polymer.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: Hereon then suggested that fixed site carrier membranes (or facilitated transport) would be a good option for future products due to their high nature of CO2/N2 selectivity. This would enable capturing CO2 at a much lower concentration and/or having much less footprint. They mentioned that the current amine-based compound needs intensive pre-treatment, and they would choose other compounds such as quaternized ammonium or silica amino compounds, which require less pre-treatment.
texts.text: Hereon then suggested that fixed site carrier membranes (or facilitated transport) would be a good option for future products due to their high nature of CO2/N2 selectivity. This would enable capturing CO2 at a much lower concentration and/or having much less footprint. They mentioned that the current amine-based compound needs intensive pre-treatment, and they would choose other compounds such as quaternized ammonium or silica amino compounds, which require less pre-treatment.
